title: తెనాలి, మట్టి మందు మహిమ
moral: నిజమైన తెలివి గొప్ప పేర్లలోనో, గంభీరమైన మాటల్లోనో ఉండదు పిల్లలూ, స్పష్టమైన ఆలోచనలో, మంచి మనసులోనే ఉంటుంది.
main_character: Tenali Ramakrishna was a witty and intelligent poet-jester in the court of Emperor Krishnadevaraya of the Vijayanagara Empire. He was known for his quick thinking, sharp humor, and ability to solve complex problems with simple, clever solutions. Always the same character across every scene.
antagonist: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The grand royal court of Vijayanagara, adorned with intricate stone carvings, vibrant tapestries, and tall, polished pillars. The air is often filled with the scent of jasmine and incense, and the distant murmur of courtly life. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: విజయనగర రాజదర్బారులో తెనాలి రామకృష్ణుడు తన చమత్కారమైన మాటలతో అందరినీ నవ్విస్తున్నాడు. పిల్లలూ, ఆ దర్బారు గోడల నిండా చక్కటి చెక్కడాలు, రంగురంగుల తెరలు వేలాడుతూ ఉండేవి. మల్లెపూల దండల సువాసన అప్పుడప్పుడు గాలిలో తేలియాడుతూ ఉండేది, తెలుసా? ఇంతలో కొత్తగా వచ్చిన రాజవైద్యుడు తన గుండెల మీద చేతులు వేసుకుని, గొప్పగా "నా మాయా మందుతో, ఏ రోగమైనా మాయం!" అంటూ అరిచాడు. అతని ముఖం గర్వంతో ఉబ్బిపోయింది. తెనాలి రామకృష్ణుడు పెదవుల మీద చిరునవ్వుతో అతని వైపు చూశాడు — కానీ ఒక్క మాట కూడా మాట్లాడలేదు. ఇప్పుడు తెనాలి ఈ డాంబికపు వైద్యుడిని ప్రశ్నిస్తాడా, లేక తన పని తాను చూసుకుంటాడా?
scenes.image_prompt: The new physician stands with his hands crossed over his chest, loudly proclaiming the power of his medicine. His face is puffed up with pride, while the other man watches him with a slight smile on his lips. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. Bright, warm morning sunlight streaming through the open arches of the court, illuminating the rich colours of the tapestries.
scenes.scene_visual: The new physician stands with his hands crossed over his chest, loudly proclaiming the power of his medicine. His face is puffed up with pride, while the other man watches him with a slight smile on his lips.
scenes.text_te_en: Vijayanagara Raajadarbaarulo Tenaali Raamakrrishnudu Tana Chamatkaaramaina Maatalato Andarini Navvistunnaadu. Pillalu, Aa Darbaaru Godala Nindaa Chakkati Chekkadaalu, Rangurangula Teralu Velaadutu Undevi. Mallepula Dandala Suvaasana Appudappudu Gaalilo Teliyaadutu Undedi, Telusaa? Intalo Kottagaa Vachchina Raajavaidyudu Tana Gundela Mida Chetulu Vesukuni, Goppagaa "Naa Maayaa Manduto, E Rogamainaa Maayan!" Antu Arichaadu. Atani Mukham Garvanto Ubbipoyindi. Tenaali Raamakrrishnudu Pedavula Mida Chirunavvuto Atani Vaipu Chushaadu — Kaani Okka Maata Kudaa Maatlaadaledu. Ippudu Tenaali I Daambikapu Vaidyudini Prashnistaadaa, Leka Tana Pani Taanu Chusukuntaadaa?
scenes.text_en: In the royal court of Vijayanagara, Tenali Ramakrishna was making everyone laugh with his witty words. Children, the walls of that court were filled with beautiful carvings, and colorful curtains hung everywhere. The sweet scent of jasmine garlands sometimes floated in the air, did you know? Just then, a new royal physician, placing his hands on his chest, proudly shouted, "With my magic medicine, any disease will disappear!" His face swelled with pride. Tenali Ramakrishna looked at him with a slight smile on his lips — but didn't say a single word. Now, will Tenali question this boastful doctor, or will he mind his own business?

scenes.text: వైద్యుడు ఒక దగ్గుతున్న సేవకుడికి తన మాయా మందులో నుంచి కొద్దిగా ఇచ్చాడు. ఆ వైద్యుడు అటూ ఇటూ చేతులు ఊపుతూ తిరుగుతుంటే, అతని మెడలో ఉన్న బంగారు గొలుసులు 'కిణ్‌కిణ్‌' మని శబ్దం చేశాయి. సేవకుడు ఒక్కసారిగా గట్టిగా కేక వేశాడు: "అయ్యో, మహారాజా! నా దగ్గు మాయమైపోయింది!" రాజు ఆనందంతో చప్పట్లు కొట్టాడు, అతని ముఖం మెరిసిపోయింది. వైద్యుడు తన పెదవుల చివర నవ్వుతో, విజయగర్వంతో నిలబడ్డాడు. తెనాలి మాత్రం కనుబొమ్మ ఎగరేసి, నిశ్శబ్దంగా ఆలోచిస్తున్నాడు — ఈ మందు నిజంగా పనిచేస్తుందా, లేక దీని వెనుక ఏదైనా మోసం దాగి ఉందా?
scenes.image_prompt: The physician stands triumphantly, a smile playing on his lips, while the king claps his hands in joy. The other man raises an eyebrow, silently observing the scene with a thoughtful expression. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. The midday sun is high, casting sharp shadows from the court pillars, the atmosphere is celebratory and bright.
scenes.scene_visual: The physician stands triumphantly, a smile playing on his lips, while the king claps his hands in joy. The other man raises an eyebrow, silently observing the scene with a thoughtful expression.
scenes.text_te_en: Vaidyudu Oka Daggutunna Sevakudiki Tana Maayaa Mandulo Nunchi Koddigaa Ichchaadu. Aa Vaidyudu Atu Itu Chetulu Uputu Tirugutunte, Atani Medalo Unna Bangaaru Golusulu 'Kin‌Kin‌' Mani Shabdam Cheshaayi. Sevakudu Okkasaarigaa Gattigaa Keka Veshaadu: "Ayyo, Mahaaraajaa! Naa Daggu Maayamaipoyindi!" Raaju Aanandanto Chappatlu Kottaadu, Atani Mukham Merisipoyindi. Vaidyudu Tana Pedavula Chivara Navvuto, Vijayagarvanto Nilabaddaadu. Tenaali Maatram Kanubomma Egaresi, Nishshabdangaa Aalochistunnaadu — I Mandu Nijangaa Panichestundaa, Leka Dini Venuka Edainaa Mosam Daagi Undaa?
scenes.text_en: The physician gave a little of his magic medicine to a coughing servant. As the physician walked around, waving his hands, the golden chains around his neck jingled 'kinkink'. The servant suddenly cried out loudly: "Oh, Maharaja! My cough has disappeared!" The king clapped with joy, his face shining brightly. The physician stood with a smile at the corner of his lips, full of triumph. Tenali, however, raised an eyebrow and thought silently — does this medicine really work, or is there some trick hidden behind it?

scenes.text: వైద్యుడు మరింత ధైర్యం తెచ్చుకుని, తన ఛాతీని ఉబ్బించి, దర్బారులో అందరినీ కలియచూశాడు. ఒక్కసారిగా ఆ దర్బారులో నిశ్శబ్దం అలుముకుంది, పట్టు వస్త్రాల సవ్వడి తప్ప ఇంకేమీ వినబడడం లేదు. అతని పెదవులపై ఒక చిన్న వెటకారపు నవ్వు మెరిసింది — ప్రత్యేకంగా తెనాలి వైపు చూస్తూ, "ఎవరైనా దీని శక్తిని ప్రశ్నించగలరా?" అని సవాలు విసిరాడు. తెనాలి రామకృష్ణుడు మొదట ఆశ్చర్యపోయినా, అతని కళ్ళల్లో ఒక దృఢ నిశ్చయం మెరిసింది. ఇప్పుడు తెనాలి ఈ ప్రత్యక్ష సవాలుకు ఎలా బదులిస్తాడు?
scenes.image_prompt: The physician puffs out his chest and scans the court, a mocking smile on his lips as he challenges everyone. He looks directly at the other man, whose initial surprise gives way to a glint of firm resolve in his eyes. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. A tense, watchful light in the court, shadows deepening slightly as the afternoon progresses, highlighting the Physician's smug face.
scenes.scene_visual: The physician puffs out his chest and scans the court, a mocking smile on his lips as he challenges everyone. He looks directly at the other man, whose initial surprise gives way to a glint of firm resolve in his eyes.
scenes.text_te_en: Vaidyudu Marinta Dhairyam Techchukuni, Tana Chaatini Ubbinchi, Darbaarulo Andarini Kaliyachushaadu. Okkasaarigaa Aa Darbaarulo Nishshabdam Alumukundi, Pattu Vastraala Savvadi Tappa Inkemi Vinabadadam Ledu. Atani Pedavulapai Oka Chinna Vetakaarapu Navvu Merisindi — Pratyekangaa Tenaali Vaipu Chustu, "Evarainaa Dini Shaktini Prashninchagalaraa?" Ani Savaalu Visiraadu. Tenaali Raamakrrishnudu Modata Aashcharyapoyinaa, Atani Kallallo Oka Drridha Nishchayam Merisindi. Ippudu Tenaali I Pratyaksha Savaaluku Elaa Badulistaadu?
scenes.text_en: The physician gathered more courage, puffed out his chest, and looked around at everyone in the court. Suddenly, silence fell over the court; nothing could be heard except the rustle of silk clothes. A small sarcastic smile gleamed on his lips — specifically looking at Tenali, he challenged, "Can anyone question its power?" Tenali Ramakrishna was surprised at first, but a firm determination shone in his eyes. Now, how will Tenali respond to this direct challenge?

scenes.text: సవాలును స్వీకరించిన తెనాలి రామకృష్ణుడు ఆ రోజంతా తన ఇంట్లోనే ఉండిపోయాడు. అతను వంటగదిలోకి వెళ్లి, చింతపండును నీళ్ళలో వేసి, మెల్లగా కలిపాడు — ఆ పుల్లని వాసన గది అంతా నిండిపోయింది. కూరగాయలు, మసాలాలు కలిపి, ఒక వింత మిశ్రమాన్ని సిద్ధం చేస్తూ, ఏదో పెద్ద మందు తయారుచేస్తున్నట్టుగా నటించాడు. ముఖం మీద ఏకాగ్రత, పెదవుల మీద చిన్న నవ్వుతో, "ఈ మాయా మందు, మాయల మందుకన్నా గొప్పది!" అని గొణిగాడు. తన మామూలు పదార్థాలను గొప్ప మాయా మందుగా దర్బారులో ఎలా చూపిస్తాడు?
scenes.image_prompt: The man stands in his kitchen, stirring tamarind in water and mixing vegetables and spices to create a strange concoction. He has a look of concentration on his face and a small smile on his lips as he mutters to himself. Warm, golden afternoon light filtering into Tenali's humble courtyard kitchen, dappling shadows across his focused work.
scenes.scene_visual: The man stands in his kitchen, stirring tamarind in water and mixing vegetables and spices to create a strange concoction. He has a look of concentration on his face and a small smile on his lips as he mutters to himself.
scenes.text_te_en: Savaalunu Svikarinchina Tenaali Raamakrrishnudu Aa Rojantaa Tana Intlone Undipoyaadu. Atanu Vantagadiloki Velli, Chintapandunu Nillalo Vesi, Mellagaa Kalipaadu — Aa Pullani Vaasana Gadi Antaa Nindipoyindi. Kuragaayalu, Masaalaalu Kalipi, Oka Vinta Mishramaanni Siddham Chestu, Edo Pedda Mandu Tayaaruchestunnattugaa Natinchaadu. Mukham Mida Ekaagrata, Pedavula Mida Chinna Navvuto, "I Maayaa Mandu, Maayala Mandukannaa Goppadi!" Ani Gonigaadu. Tana Maamulu Padaarthaalanu Goppa Maayaa Mandugaa Darbaarulo Elaa Chupistaadu?
scenes.text_en: Accepting the challenge, Tenali Ramakrishna stayed home all day. He went into the kitchen, put tamarind in water, and slowly mixed it — the sour smell filled the entire room. Mixing vegetables and spices, he prepared a strange mixture, pretending to make some great medicine. With concentration on his face and a small smile on his lips, he muttered, "This magic medicine is greater than any other magic medicine!" How will he present his ordinary ingredients as a great magic medicine in the court?

scenes.text: మరుసటి రోజు తెనాలి దర్బారులోకి ప్రశాంతంగా అడుగు పెట్టాడు, అతని చేతిలో ఒక చిన్న సీసా ఉంది. అతని 'మందు' నుండి పుల్లగా, తీయగా ఒక వింత వాసన గాలిలో తేలియాడుతోంది. వైద్యుడు తెనాలిని చూసి మొదట అయోమయంగా చూశాడు, తర్వాత అతని ముఖంలో ఆందోళన కనిపించింది. తెనాలి నిలబడి, వైద్యుడి కళ్ళల్లోకి సూటిగా చూస్తూ, "వైద్యుడు తన మందును ముందుగా రుచి చూడాలి, ఇది ఆచారము!" అని చెప్పాడు. వైద్యుడి చేతులు ఒక్కసారిగా వణికాయి — అతను ఇప్పుడు తెనాలి మందును తాగుతాడా, లేక తన భయాన్ని బయటపెడతాడా?
scenes.image_prompt: The man stands calmly in the court, holding a small bottle, and looks directly into the physician's eyes. The physician's hands tremble as he faces the challenge, his face showing clear anxiety. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. Bright, expectant light in the court, focusing on the two figures, creating a spotlight effect on the small bowl in Tenali's hand.
scenes.scene_visual: The man stands calmly in the court, holding a small bottle, and looks directly into the physician's eyes. The physician's hands tremble as he faces the challenge, his face showing clear anxiety.
scenes.text_te_en: Marusati Roju Tenaali Darbaaruloki Prashaantangaa Adugu Pettaadu, Atani Chetilo Oka Chinna Sisaa Undi. Atani 'Mandu' Nundi Pullagaa, Tiyagaa Oka Vinta Vaasana Gaalilo Teliyaadutondi. Vaidyudu Tenaalini Chusi Modata Ayomayangaa Chushaadu, Tarvaata Atani Mukhanlo Aandolana Kanipinchindi. Tenaali Nilabadi, Vaidyudi Kallalloki Sutigaa Chustu, "Vaidyudu Tana Mandunu Mundugaa Ruchi Chudaali, Idi Aachaaramu!" Ani Cheppaadu. Vaidyudi Chetulu Okkasaarigaa Vanikaayi — Atanu Ippudu Tenaali Mandunu Taagutaadaa, Leka Tana Bhayaanni Bayatapedataadaa?
scenes.text_en: The next day, Tenali calmly entered the court, a small bottle in his hand. A strange sour and sweet smell floated in the air from his 'medicine'. The physician first looked at Tenali with confusion, then anxiety appeared on his face. Tenali stood, looking directly into the physician's eyes, and said, "A physician must taste his own medicine first; this is the custom!" The physician's hands trembled suddenly — will he drink Tenali's medicine now, or will he reveal his fear?

scenes.text: వైద్యుడు చెమటలు కక్కుతూ, ముఖం పాలిపోయి, తెనాలి మందును తాగడానికి చాలా సాకులు చెప్పాడు. అతని వేళ్ళు పట్టు వస్త్రాలపై నెమ్మదిగా కదిలాయి. "ఇది చాలా శక్తివంతమైనది, నా ఆరోగ్యానికి మంచిది కాదు!" అని కేక వేశాడు. తెనాలి మాత్రం శాంతంగా, తెలివైన నవ్వుతో అతని వైపు చూస్తూ నిలబడ్డాడు. వైద్యుడు ఆ మందును రుచి చూడకుండా తప్పించుకోగలడా, లేక తెనాలి తెలివి అతన్ని చిక్కుల్లో పడేస్తుందా?
scenes.image_prompt: The physician, pale and sweating, makes frantic excuses to avoid drinking the medicine, his fingers nervously tracing patterns on his silk robes. The other man stands calmly, watching him with a knowing, intelligent smile. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. A tense, slightly shadowed atmosphere in the court, highlighting the Physician's pale face and nervous movements, contrasting with Tenali's calm presence.
scenes.scene_visual: The physician, pale and sweating, makes frantic excuses to avoid drinking the medicine, his fingers nervously tracing patterns on his silk robes. The other man stands calmly, watching him with a knowing, intelligent smile.
scenes.text_te_en: Vaidyudu Chematalu Kakkutu, Mukham Paalipoyi, Tenaali Mandunu Taagadaaniki Chaalaa Saakulu Cheppaadu. Atani Vellu Pattu Vastraalapai Nemmadigaa Kadilaayi. "Idi Chaalaa Shaktivantamainadi, Naa Aarogyaaniki Manchidi Kaadu!" Ani Keka Veshaadu. Tenaali Maatram Shaantangaa, Telivaina Navvuto Atani Vaipu Chustu Nilabaddaadu. Vaidyudu Aa Mandunu Ruchi Chudakundaa Tappinchukogaladaa, Leka Tenaali Telivi Atanni Chikkullo Padestundaa?
scenes.text_en: The physician, sweating and pale-faced, made many excuses to avoid drinking Tenali's medicine. His fingers slowly moved over his silk clothes. "This is too powerful, it's not good for my health!" he cried out. Tenali, however, stood calmly, looking at him with a wise smile. Could the physician escape without tasting the medicine, or would Tenali's cleverness trap him?

scenes.text: తెనాలి ఒక చిరునవ్వు నవ్వి, తన సీసాలోని ద్రవాన్ని అందరికీ చూపించాడు. దర్బారులోని సేవకులందరూ ఒక్కసారిగా ఆశ్చర్యంతో నోరు తెరిచారు. "నా మందులో మిరియాలు, చింతపండు మాత్రమే ఉన్నాయి! మీ భయం మీ మోసాన్ని బయటపెట్టింది!" అని గంభీరంగా ప్రకటించాడు. రాజవైద్యుడు తలదించుకుని, అతని భుజాలు కుంగిపోయాయి — అతని ముఖం అవమానంతో ఎర్రబారింది. తెనాలి తెలివి, నిజమైన వైద్యం కేవలం నిజాయితీలోనే ఉంటుందని నిరూపించింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The man smiles and holds up his bottle, revealing its contents to the astonished court. The physician hangs his head, his shoulders slumped, and his face red with shame. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. A dramatic, revealing light from above, shining directly on Tenali and the Physician, making Tenali seem illuminated with truth and the Physician cast in shadow.
scenes.scene_visual: The man smiles and holds up his bottle, revealing its contents to the astonished court. The physician hangs his head, his shoulders slumped, and his face red with shame.
scenes.text_te_en: Tenaali Oka Chirunavvu Navvi, Tana Sisaaloni Dravaanni Andariki Chupinchaadu. Darbaaruloni Sevakulandaru Okkasaarigaa Aashcharyanto Noru Terichaaru. "Naa Mandulo Miriyaalu, Chintapandu Maatrame Unnaayi! Mi Bhayam Mi Mosaanni Bayatapettindi!" Ani Gambhirangaa Prakatinchaadu. Raajavaidyudu Taladinchukuni, Atani Bhujaalu Kungipoyaayi — Atani Mukham Avamaananto Errabaarindi. Tenaali Telivi, Nijamaina Vaidyam Kevalam Nijaayitilone Untundani Nirupinchindi.
scenes.text_en: Tenali smiled slightly and showed everyone the liquid in his bottle. All the servants in the court gasped in surprise. "My medicine only contains pepper and tamarind! Your fear has exposed your deception!" he declared solemnly. The royal physician hung his head, his shoulders slumped — his face turned red with shame. Tenali's cleverness proved that true medicine lies only in honesty.

scenes.text: రాజు కృష్ణదేవరాయలు తెనాలి తెలివికి ముగ్ధుడై, పగలబడి నవ్వాడు. ఆ పెద్ద దర్బారు హాలు అంతా సంతోషపు నవ్వులతో నిండిపోయింది. "రామకృష్ణా, నీ తెలివికి సాటి లేదు!" అంటూ తెనాలిని ప్రశంసించాడు. వెంటనే ఆ మోసగాడైన వైద్యుడిని దర్బారు నుండి పంపించేశాడు. తెనాలి మాత్రం వినయంగా నవ్వుతూ, రాజుకు వంగి నమస్కరించాడు — తన పని పూర్తయ్యిందని అతనికి తెలుసు.
scenes.image_prompt: The king throws his head back and laughs heartily, filling the grand court hall with joyous sounds. The man bows humbly to the king, a modest smile on his face, knowing his task is complete. Also in this scene: The Royal Physician, Vaidya Vishwasa, was a portly man with a long, neatly combed grey beard and a perpetually sneering expression. He wore opulent silk robes of deep maroon and gold, adorned with numerous gold chains and rings, and always carried a carved wooden staff topped with a gemstone. Always the same character across every scene. The court bathed in a warm, golden, late afternoon light, creating an atmosphere of peace, justice, and joyful celebration.
scenes.scene_visual: The king throws his head back and laughs heartily, filling the grand court hall with joyous sounds. The man bows humbly to the king, a modest smile on his face, knowing his task is complete.
scenes.text_te_en: Raaju Krrishnadevaraayalu Tenaali Teliviki Mugdhudai, Pagalabadi Navvaadu. Aa Pedda Darbaaru Haalu Antaa Santoshapu Navvulato Nindipoyindi. "Raamakrrishnaa, Ni Teliviki Saati Ledu!" Antu Tenaalini Prashansinchaadu. Ventane Aa Mosagaadaina Vaidyudini Darbaaru Nundi Pampincheshaadu. Tenaali Maatram Vinayangaa Navvutu, Raajuku Vangi Namaskarinchaadu — Tana Pani Purtayyindani Ataniki Telusu.
scenes.text_en: King Krishnadevaraya was captivated by Tenali's wit and burst out laughing. The entire grand court hall filled with happy laughter. "Ramakrishna, your cleverness is unmatched!" he praised Tenali. Immediately, he sent the deceptive physician away from the court. Tenali, however, smiled humbly and bowed to the king — he knew his work was done.

supporting_characters.description: Emperor Krishnadevaraya was a majestic and benevolent ruler, known for his wisdom and patronage of arts and literature. He had a regal bearing, often seen in rich emerald green and gold attire, with a keen, discerning gaze. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: This story shines in its ability to engage a child, with a warm, oral storytelling style, vivid emotional descriptions, and excellent use of suspense hooks. The dialogue is natural and character-specific, making the scenes come alive. However, the explicit statement of the moral at the very beginning significantly weakens the narrative arc, as it preaches the lesson rather than allowing it to emerge organically. The single most important fix to reach a 9+ would be to remove the pre-stated moral and let the story's events convey its wisdom.

moral_te_en: Nijamaina Telivi Goppa Perlalono, Gambhiramaina Maatallono Undadu Pillalu, Spashtamaina Aalochanalo, Manchi Manasulone Untundi.
title_en: Tenali, and the Magic of the Mud Medicine
moral_en: True wisdom, children, is not found in grand names or fancy words. It lies in clear thinking and a good heart.
